sections.paragraph:
m. followed by Makk. חָק־, with suff. חֻקִּי, but חָקְךָ Lev 10:13 חָקְכֶם Exo 5:14 plur. חֻקִּים, constr. חֻקֵּי and חוּקֵּי Eze 20:18, properly that which is established or definite (from the root חָקַק No. 3), e.g. חֻקִּי that which is appointed for me, Job 23:14. Specially-

(a) an appointed portion of labour, a task, Exo 5:14 Pro 31:15 of food, Pro 30:8.

(b) a defined limit, a bound, Job 26:10 Pro 8:29. לִבְלִי חֹק without limit, Isa 5:14 24:5 .

(c) an appointed time, Job 14:13 38:26 .

(d) an appointed law, a statute, an ordinance, Gen 47:26 Exo 12:24 used of the laws of nature [as prescribed by God], Job 28:26 of laws given by God to man, Deu 4:5, , 14 6:24 11:32 12:1 a decree of God, Psa 2:7 a custom observed as though it were a law, Jud. 11:39 right, privilege, observed as though it had been a law, Exo 29:28 [This passage speaks of an actual ordinance of God].
